response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack web and application developer with 15+ years of experience, but he is a near-total mismatch for this role. The single hardest requirement — native-level Japanese proficiency — is entirely absent from his profile; he lists English, Romanian, and basic German only. Beyond that disqualifying gap, his background is overwhelmingly in TypeScript, Node.js, and web platforms with no evidence of Linux kernel development, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware. The only C/C++ exposure is a brief six-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015, which does not constitute the deep UNIX C proficiency required.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development covering web, mobile, and some embedded/hardware work (robotic arms at Altom, audio synthesis in C++ for thesis)
Demonstrated high-performance systems engineering at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
English fluency satisfies the documentation reading and email communication requirement
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No experience with network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC auth), L2/L3 switching, or embedded networking firmware
C programming is minimal and dated (one six-month role in 2014–2015); no Linux kernel source investigation or modification work anywhere in the history

key_factors.factor: No network security or embedded firmware domain experience
Long general software engineering track record
